;<span id="U-Factor">U-Factor</span>
:number associated with each [[Glossary:Memorized_element|memorized element]]. It equals to the ratio of the current [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] and the previously used interval. If the [[Glossary:Element|element]] has not been repeated yet, i.e. the previous [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] is not defined, '''U-Factor''' equals the first interval. The greater the '''U-Factor''' the greater the increase of the [[Glossary:Interval|interval]] between [[Glossary:Repetition|repetitions]]. For [[Glossary:Item|items]], '''U-Factors''' are determined by [https://supermemo.guru/wiki/SuperMemo_Algorithm SuperMemo Algorithm]. For [[Glossary:Topic|topics]], '''U-Factors''' are determined by [[Glossary:A-Factor|A-Factors]]. For [[Glossary:Topic|topics]] that have already been reviewed at least once, that have not been subject to postpones, '''U-Factors''' equal [[Glossary:A-Factor|A-Factors]]. ''U'' in U-Factor stands for ''used interval increase''
